  "decision": "fail",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Sheave misalignment detected via straightedge method",
    "Misalignment exceeds OEM tolerance for belt drive systems"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lock out and tag out equipment",
    "Loosen sheave mounting bolts",
    "Realign sheaves using straightedge and feeler gauges per manufacturer specification",
    "Re-torque mounting bolts to specified value",
    "Verify alignment after tightening"
  ],
  "rationale": "Visible sheave misalignment in a belt drive system causes accelerated belt and sheave wear, vibration, and potential coupling failure. Manufacturer guides typically specify alignment tolerances (e.g., 1/2 degree angular or 0.5 mm offset per 100 mm sheave diameter). The observed condition is visibly out of tolerance, posing a high risk of premature failure and safety hazard if operated.",
  "references": [
    "OEM belt drive installation and alignment specifications",
    "ANSI/AGMA 9005-E02 Industrial Gear Lubrication (alignment principles)",
    "ISO 10816-3 Mechanical vibration — Evaluation of machine vibration by measurements on non-rotating parts"
  ]
}
